title: MySQL Error 1153: Got a Packet Bigger Than 'max_allowed_packet'
description: Your import fails with ERROR 1153. Here's why it happens and how to fix it without server access.
author: SQLSplit

content.text: ERROR 1153 means MySQL received a statement larger than it allows. Imports die mid‑way, often on big INSERT blocks.
MySQL limits the size of a single statement. Large dumps often contain huge INSERT statements that exceed max_allowed_packet.

items: Split the dump into smaller files

content.text: Splitting reduces statement size and avoids packet limits. See also: **MySQL max_allowed_packet Error: What It Means and How to Fix It**.

content.title: Split your SQL dump
content.text: Break huge INSERTs into safe chunks.

faq.question: Do I need server access to fix Error 1153?
faq.answer: No. Splitting the dump avoids oversized statements without changing server settings.

faq.question: What size should I aim for?
faq.answer: Start with 10–20MB chunks for phpMyAdmin or shared hosting.
